contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: deploy_gas_project identity check (PR-1g)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/tokenomics/pull/324
  
  Details:
  deploy_gas_project — identity check (PR-1g), 2026-05-29.
  
  Closes the 'ensure 1zKgMwd6 always deploys as admin@truesight.me' constraint Gary raised after confirming the unminted scriptId belongs to admin@. Generalises owner_email from PR-1d into a per-project identity-pinning constraint that the deploy script enforces.
  
  PRs delivered:
  
  1. tokenomics#324 — Before any clasp push, resolve_clasp_identity() reads ~/.clasprc.json (or CLASPRC_PATH env override), exchanges the refresh_token for an access token via oauth2.googleapis.com, calls oauth2/v3/userinfo, gets the email. Comparison: case-insensitive against project block owner_email. Mismatch + --push refuses with exit 1 and a clear 'switch accounts' message naming both emails and the three fix options (clasp logout + login, CLASPRC_PATH per-account file, or --allow-identity-mismatch override). Mismatch + dry-run warns loudly. Identity unresolved (no clasprc / network down) doesn't block dry-run. New CLI: --allow-identity-mismatch. New env: CLASPRC_PATH for per-account credentials files. docs/gas_deploy_workflow.md gains an 'Identity pinning' section documenting the constraint and the multi-account workflow. Smoke-tested with three cases: matched gary@/gary@ (clean), admin@/gary@ dry-run (warns), admin@/gary@ --push (refuses, exit 1).
  
  This means autopilot's gas_deploy_project tool now also inherits the identity check — when admin@'s 1MnAsIQA project gets pushed via autopilot, EC2's clasp identity must match. If EC2 is logged in as gary@, autopilot can't accidentally push to admin@-owned projects, and the inverse holds. The constraint sticks regardless of who's invoking the deploy script.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 50.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
